content:
- `task_switch.sh` completed and rotated the Ralph summaries; `.ralph/current_task.txt` was removed as part of the switch, which is expected.
- Next I am staging all files, committing with the task-finished message plus test evidence, and then pushing the branch.

- After the task-finished commit, the Ralph summary files regenerated once more with later timestamps only.
- I am committing that tiny follow-up refresh so the branch is clean before the final push.

- Started Choose-Task pass.
- Read prior progress log and loaded the list-active-tasks skill instructions.
- Next I am refreshing Ralph task summaries and reading the task selection inputs.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and read current_tasks plus current_tasks_done.
- Found no active bug tasks; the first unblocked failing task appears to be story-02 task-01 because story-01 foundation tasks are already done.
- Next I am briefly checking the codebase stack and the selected task file, then I will write .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Chose the next Ralph task and wrote .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Selection result: .ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-01-benchmark-option-model.md.
- Reason: no active bug tasks, and this is the only unblocked failing task because its blocker story-01/task-03 already passes true in current_tasks_done.

- Read previous Ralph progress log and confirmed the selected task is .ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-01-benchmark-option-model.md.
- Loaded the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions for this turn.
- Next I am reading the task file and determining whether this is a planning-only pass or an execution pass.

- Read task-01-benchmark-option-model.md; it is still in the initial state with no plan marker.
- This means the required action for this turn is to create a separate plan file, leave it at TO BE VERIFIED, record its path, and stop.
- Next I am checking existing Ralph plan-file conventions and doing the required online benchmark-tool research so the plan is concrete.

- Found the Ralph plan-file convention: task files record a <plan> path, and plan files end with either TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE.
- Confirmed this turn should add a new plan file under the story-specific _plans directory and link it from the task file.
- Next I am reading the current Go package boundaries plus doing the required benchmark-tool research so the plan matches the existing architecture.

- Read the current Go packages and the downstream story-02 task files.
- The benchmark option model will sit between HTTP and the run coordinator, so the plan needs one canonical domain owner instead of request structs becoming the source of truth.
- Next I am finishing the required benchmark-tool research and then writing the task plan plus task-file <plan> link.

- Created the planning artifact for story-02 task-01 at .ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-01-benchmark-option-model_plans/2026-04-30-benchmark-option-model-plan.md.
- Linked that plan from .ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-01-benchmark-option-model.md and left the plan ending at TO BE VERIFIED, as required for the planning-only pass.
- The plan chooses internal/benchmark as the single canonical owner of start/alter JSON options, defaults, validation, and unknown-field rejection; next turn should review the proposed flat JSON shape and either promote it to NOW EXECUTE or reopen the design immediately.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill docs.
- Confirmed the selected task is story-02 task-01 benchmark option model and that the previous pass stopped with a linked plan in TO BE VERIFIED state.
- Next I am reviewing the task file and linked plan to decide whether to promote the design to NOW EXECUTE or keep it in verification.

- Reviewed the linked benchmark option model plan against the current Go package layout, the improve-code-boundaries smell guidance, and online benchmark-tool docs.
- Confirmed the design is stable: one new internal/benchmark package should own flat JSON decoding, unknown-field rejection, defaults, and validation for start/alter options without introducing duplicate DTO layers.
- Promoted the plan file to NOW EXECUTE at .ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-01-benchmark-option-model_plans/2026-04-30-benchmark-option-model-plan.md; next turn must read that marker and execute the red-green slices from the plan.

- Confirmed the active task is story-02 task-01 benchmark option model and that its linked plan ends with NOW EXECUTE.
- Re-read the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions and the task acceptance criteria.
- Next I am reading the current Go package layout and tests so I can implement the first red-green slice in a new internal/benchmark package without introducing duplicate DTOs.

- Implemented internal/benchmark as the canonical owner of benchmark start/alter option decoding, defaults, and validation.
- Added red-green coverage for start defaults, unknown-field rejection, transaction-profile defaulting, start validation, and alter allowed/rejected fields.
- Next I am reviewing the new package for boundary cleanup and then running formatting plus make check / make test.

- Formatted the new benchmark package and passed the required repository gates `make check` and `make test`.
- No extra e2e or long lane was run because this is a normal task and the task does not require it.
- Next I am running `make lint` explicitly, then updating the Ralph task file to passes true, switching tasks, and creating the required commit/push.
